---
title: "Arrow Shortcode Demo"
format: html
---

{{< arrow from="850,100" to="1120,20" control1="1050,120" color="hotpink" width="3" position="absolute" >}}

## Basic Arrows

Straight line (no control points):

{{< arrow from="50,50" to="250,50" >}}

## Quadratic Bezier (one control point)

Curve bending upward:

{{< arrow from="50,100" to="250,100" control1="150,20" >}}

Curve bending downward:

{{< arrow from="50,50" to="250,50" control1="150,130" >}}

## Cubic Bezier (two control points)

S-curve:

{{< arrow from="50,50" to="250,150" control1="150,0" control2="150,200" >}}

## Styling Options

Red arrow:

{{< arrow from="50,50" to="250,50" control1="150,120" color="red" >}}

Thick blue arrow:

{{< arrow from="50,50" to="250,80" control1="150,150" color="blue" width="4" >}}

Larger arrowhead:

{{< arrow from="50,50" to="250,50" control1="150,120" color="green" size="15" >}}

## Positioned Arrows

Use `position="absolute"` for document-level positioning (scrolls with page):

```markdown
{{</* arrow from="600,80" to="780,15" control1="750,100" position="absolute" */>}}
```

Use `position="fixed"` for viewport-fixed positioning (stays in place while scrolling):

```markdown
{{</* arrow from="100,100" to="300,50" position="fixed" */>}}
```

Use `position="absolute"` inside a `position: relative` container for scoped positioning:

::: {style="position: relative; height: 200px; border: 1px dashed #ccc;"}

This container scopes the absolute positioning.

{{< arrow from="50,50" to="300,150" control1="175,20" color="purple" position="absolute" >}}

:::
